channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Ted Turner, CNN Founder and Media Pioneer, Dies at 87
item.description: The billionaire media mogul who revolutionized cable news with the launch of CNN in 1980 has died. Turner also owned the Atlanta Braves and was a champion yachtsman, leaving an indelible mark on both media and sports.

item.title: France Deploys Aircraft Carrier Toward Strait of Hormuz
item.description: France moved its Charles de Gaulle carrier strike group to the Red Sea in preparation for a potential mission to secure the Strait of Hormuz. The move comes as Washington and Tehran negotiate a possible peace deal.

item.title: Japan Nikkei Hits Record High Above 62,000 on Middle East Peace Hopes
item.description: Japans benchmark Nikkei 225 surged past 62,000 for the first time ever, driven by strong tech earnings and growing optimism about a potential US-Iran peace deal. Asian stocks broadly rallied on the news.

item.title: Venezuela Rejects ICJ Jurisdiction in Guyana Oil Territory Dispute
item.description: Venezuela told the UN International Court of Justice it has no jurisdiction over its century-old dispute with Guyana concerning the oil-rich Essequibo region. Venezuela claims the territory was fraudulently taken in the colonial era.

item.title: ECB Rate Hike Chances Rise Amid Inflation Pressures
item.description: European Central Bank board member Piero Cipollone said the probability of an ECB rate hike has increased as inflation pressures remain elevated. The Iran war has complicated the global easing cycle.

item.title: North Korea Drops Reunification Goal in Constitutional Shift
item.description: Pyongyang has removed references to "national reunification" and redefined inter-Korean borders in its revised constitution, signaling a potential move toward peaceful co-existence with South Korea.

item.title: US Counterterrorism Strategy Adds Offensive Cyber Operations
item.description: The Trump administration unveiled a new counterterrorism strategy that includes offensive cyber operations against groups deemed threats to US interests, marking a significant expansion of American counterterrorism tools.

item.title: Pentagon AI Adoption Gives Cyber Criminals Nation-State Powers
item.description: Pentagon leaders praise agentic AI tools that compress weeks of work into hours, but security experts warn the same tools are giving cyber criminals capabilities previously reserved for nation-states.

item.title: Trump Counterterrorism Strategy Brands Europe Terrorism Incubator
item.description: The new US counterterrorism strategy accuses Europe of fostering terrorism through mass migration while expanding domestic focus beyond Islamic extremism to include other threats.

item.title: Japan Nikkei Surges Past 61,000 on Iran Deal Optimism
item.description: Japanese stocks hit a record high as markets reopened after holidays, with optimism growing around a potential US-Iran agreement to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Russia resumes forex purchases as Iran war oil windfall boosts revenues
item.description: Russia will buy foreign currency for the first time since the Ukraine war began, as surging oil prices from the Middle East conflict have increased Moscow's crude export revenues.

item.title: Iran reviews US peace proposal as Trump signals war may be ending
item.description: Iran is reviewing a US peace proposal that would formally end the war, though key American demands on Iran's nuclear program and reopening the Strait of Hormuz remain unaddressed.

item.title: Ukraine accuses Russia of violating ceasefire proposed by Kyiv
item.description: President Zelenskiy says Russia carried out dozens of assaults, air strikes and drone attacks, demonstrating Moscow's "obvious spurning" of peace efforts.

item.title: G7 trade ministers discuss critical minerals as US-EU tariff tensions strain unity
item.description: Trade ministers from the Group of Seven sought common ground on securing critical mineral supplies dominated by China, but fresh US tariff threats against European cars risked straining alliance unity.

item.title: Japan fires missile in joint drill with US allies in Philippines near South China Sea
item.description: Japan's Self-Defense Forces fired an anti-ship missile during exercises with US, Australian and Philippine forces, demonstrating growing military cooperation in waters facing the contested South China Sea.

item.title: Gulf Economies Face Long-Term Hit from Iran Conflict
item.description: The ongoing Iran-US-Israel war is dealing a sustained blow to Gulf economies, with Iranian strikes on gas fields and shipping disruptions threatening the region economic transformation plans.

item.title: Fed Officials Warn Supply Chain Risks Fuel Inflation Concerns
item.description: Federal Reserve officials said the US-backed war with Iran is raising the risk of sustained inflation shock, with high oil prices and growing concerns about global supply chain disruptions.

item.title: Pope to Visit Canary Islands, Key Migrant Entry Point
item.description: Pope Leo will visit the Canary Islands, major entry points for migrants braving dangerous Atlantic waters, as part of a tour of Spain next month, the Vatican announced.

item.title: A Year Into Papacy, Pope Leo Finds His Voice on World Stage
item.description: Pope Leo marks his first year leading the Catholic Church with a higher public profile, having grown more outspoken on global affairs and drawn the ire of US President Donald Trump.

item.title: North Korea declares it is not bound by nuclear non-proliferation treaty
item.description: Pyongyang says the US and other countries are "tarnishing the atmosphere" at the UN NPT review conference by raising concerns about its nuclear weapons program.

item.title: Russia cancels Victory Day celebrations in Crimea over Ukraine drone threat
item.description: Russian occupation authorities in Crimea cancelled May 9 Victory Day events citing security concerns amid Ukrainian drone strikes targeting the peninsula.

item.title: Israeli forces launch new incursion into Syrias Daraa province
item.description: Israeli forces carried out an incursion in the Yarmouk Basin region in western Daraa countryside late Wednesday, marking continued Israeli operations in southern Syria.

item.title: Russias YJ-20 hypersonic missile threatens US carrier dominance
item.description: SCMP analyzes Chinas YJ-20 hypersonic sea-based missile, a highly maneuverable weapon that could sink aircraft carriers with a single hit and is nearly impossible to intercept.

item.title: UAE Rejects Iran Criticism of US Defense Partnerships
item.description: The UAE stated its international defense partnerships are a sovereign matter, dismissing Iranian claims that Abu Dhabi cooperation with the US threatens Iranian security.

item.title: Russia Warns Diplomats to Evacuate Kyiv Before May 9
item.description: Russia warned foreign diplomatic missions to evacuate staff from Kyiv if Moscow launches mass strikes in response to Ukrainian attempts to disrupt Victory Day commemorations.
